_source: Real estate investment analysis guides, Appraisal Institute, Property management standards
_note: Long-tail real estate investing. Valuation methods, deal analysis, property management, financing structures.

comparable_sales.process: Find 3-5 similar properties sold recently; adjust for differences; determine value

adjustments.location: +$10k for better neighborhood; -$5k for busy street
adjustments.size: $100-150/sq ft; adjust for square footage difference
adjustments.condition: +$15k for recent renovation; -$20k for deferred maintenance
adjustments.features: +$5k for garage; +$3k for fireplace; -$8k for no central AC

comparable_sales.best_for: Residential; single-family homes; condos; adequate comparable sales data

income_approach.noi_calculation: Gross Rental Income - Vacancy - Operating Expenses (exclude debt service and depreciation)
income_approach.cap_rate_determination: Market cap rates; 4-5% class A urban; 8-10% class C suburban; 12%+ risky/tertiary markets

gross_rent_multiplier.formula: GRM = Sale Price / Gross Annual Rent
gross_rent_multiplier.usage: Quick estimate; value = GRM × gross rent; less accurate than cap rate
gross_rent_multiplier.example: Comp sold $400k with $40k rent = GRM 10; subject has $35k rent → $35k × 10 = $350k value

cost_approach.formula: Land Value + (Replacement Cost - Depreciation) = Property Value
cost_approach.use: New construction; special use properties; insurance; not common for investors

depreciation_types.physical: Wear and tear; deferred maintenance; weather damage
depreciation_types.functional: Outdated layout; poor design; obsolete features
depreciation_types.external: Location issues; economic conditions; neighborhood decline

cash_on_cash_return.formula: Annual Pre-Tax Cash Flow / Total Cash Invested
cash_on_cash_return.example: $10k annual cash flow, $100k down payment = 10% CoC return

cap_rate.comparison: Higher cap rate = higher return but usually higher risk/lower quality

debt_service_coverage_ratio.lender_requirement: Minimum 1.20-1.25 DSCR; means NOI must be 20-25% above debt payments
debt_service_coverage_ratio.example: NOI $100k, debt service $80k = 1.25 DSCR; meets lender requirement
debt_service_coverage_ratio.below_1.0: Property doesn't cover debt; negative cash flow; lender won't approve

break_even_occupancy.formula: (Operating Expenses + Debt Service) / Gross Potential Income
break_even_occupancy.interpretation: Occupancy needed to cover expenses and debt; <85% is good; >90% is risky
break_even_occupancy.example: $70k opex + $40k debt = $110k / $150k GPI = 73% break-even; safe cushion

income.gross_potential_income: All units at market rent × 12 months
income.vacancy_allowance: 5-10% typical; higher for new property, class C, or weak market
income.other_income: Laundry, parking, pet fees, late fees, storage; 2-5% of GPI typical

operating_expenses.property_taxes: Assessed value × tax rate; often reassessed after sale; budget for increase
operating_expenses.insurance: $500-1500/unit/year; flood zone higher; claims history affects rate
operating_expenses.utilities: Common area; vacant units; tenant-paid excludes
operating_expenses.repairs_maintenance: 5-10% of EGI; $500-1000/unit/year; age and condition vary
operating_expenses.property_management: 8-10% of collected rents; even if self-managing, account for time value
operating_expenses.reserves: Capital expenditures; $250-500/unit/year; roof, HVAC, parking lot, appliances
operating_expenses.other: Trash, landscaping, pest control, legal, marketing

pro_forma_analysis.noi: EGI - Operating Expenses (no debt service, no depreciation)
pro_forma_analysis.cash_flow: NOI - Debt Service = Before-Tax Cash Flow

one_percent_rule.guideline: Monthly rent should be ≥1% of purchase price for cash flow
one_percent_rule.limitations: Rough filter; doesn't account for expenses, financing, taxes; varies by market

fifty_percent_rule.guideline: Operating expenses ≈50% of gross income (excluding debt service)
fifty_percent_rule.use: Quick estimate; actual expenses vary 35-65%; verify with detailed pro forma

residential_investment.conventional: 20-25% down; rates ~0.5-1% higher than owner-occupied; 6-10 properties max per investor
residential_investment.portfolio_lenders: 10+ properties; relationship-based; flexible terms; higher rates
residential_investment.hard_money: 12-18% rate; 2-5 points; short-term (6-24 months); asset-based (65-75% LTV); fix-and-flip

commercial_multifamily.agency_debt: Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac; 5+ units; 75-80% LTV; 10-30 year; non-recourse; 1.25 DSCR min
commercial_multifamily.life_insurance: Large loans (>$5M); low rates; prepayment penalties; 10-25 year
commercial_multifamily.cmbs: Conduit loans; securitized; 65-75% LTV; inflexible; 10 year with 25-30 amortization
commercial_multifamily.bank_portfolio: Local banks; relationship; 5-25 year; often recourse; may require other deposits/business

creative_financing.seller_financing: Seller acts as lender; negotiable terms; often higher rate but flexible; easier qualification
creative_financing.subject_to: Buy subject to existing mortgage; assume payments without bank approval; due-on-sale clause risk
creative_financing.lease_option: Lease with option to buy; builds equity via credits; seller retains title until purchase
creative_financing.wraparound: Seller keeps existing mortgage; buyer makes payment to seller; seller pays underlying mortgage; spread is seller's profit

tenant_screening.income_verification: 3x rent in gross income; paystubs, W2, tax returns, bank statements
tenant_screening.credit_check: Minimum 600 score typical; look for evictions, collections, payment history
tenant_screening.background_check: Criminal history; sex offender registry; prior evictions
tenant_screening.landlord_references: Last 2 landlords; verify rent paid on time; reason for leaving; would they rent again?
tenant_screening.employment_verification: Call employer; confirm position, salary, duration; avoid job-hoppers

lease_clauses.late_fees: $50-100 or 5-10% of rent if >5 days late; check state law limits
lease_clauses.security_deposit: 1-2 months rent; held in separate account (some states); itemized deductions within 30 days of moveout
lease_clauses.maintenance_responsibilities: Landlord: structural, major systems, code compliance; Tenant: minor repairs, cleanliness, damage they caused
lease_clauses.entry_notice: 24-48 hours required in most states; emergencies exempt
lease_clauses.pet_policy: Pet deposit $200-500; pet rent $25-50/month; breed/size restrictions; ESA/service animals exempt (FHA)

eviction_process.notice: 3-day pay or quit (non-payment); 30-day no cause (month-to-month); comply with state law
eviction_process.filing: Unlawful detainer complaint; file with court; pay filing fee $200-500
eviction_process.service: Sheriff serves tenant; must be proper service or case dismissed
eviction_process.court_hearing: 5-30 days depending on jurisdiction; bring lease, ledger, proof of notice
eviction_process.writ_of_possession: If win, sheriff removes tenant 5-10 days later; don't self-help evict (illegal)
eviction_process.timeline: 30-90 days typical; longer in tenant-friendly states (CA, NY, NJ)
eviction_process.cost: $1500-5000 total including legal, lost rent, turnover; avoid if possible via screening

maintenance_systems.preventive: HVAC service 2x/year; gutter cleaning; smoke detector testing; extends life, prevents emergency
maintenance_systems.vendor_relationships: Plumber, electrician, HVAC, handyman, cleaner; negotiate rates; fast response
maintenance_systems.emergency_protocol: 24/7 phone line; define emergency (water leak, no heat, lock out); response within 2-4 hours
maintenance_systems.capital_reserve: $250-500/unit/year; roof (20-25 years), HVAC (15-20), water heater (10-12), appliances (8-12), parking lot (20-30)

depreciation.residential: 27.5 year straight-line; building only (not land); $200k building = $7,273/year
depreciation.cost_segregation: Accelerate depreciation; classify components (flooring, lighting, landscaping) as 5/7/15 year; upfront deduction; worth it for $500k+ properties

1031_exchange.rule: Defer capital gains by exchanging into like-kind property
1031_exchange.timeline: 45 days to identify replacement; 180 days to close; strict deadlines
1031_exchange.like_kind: Any real estate for any real estate (investment/business use)
1031_exchange.qualified_intermediary: Must use QI; can't touch proceeds between sale and purchase
1031_exchange.boot: Mortgage reduction, cash received, or non-like-kind property triggers taxable gain to extent of boot

deductions.operating_expenses: All ordinary and necessary; repairs, utilities, management, insurance, taxes
deductions.travel: To/from property; 67¢/mile (2024) or actual expenses; inspect, repair, show, manage
deductions.home_office: If regular and exclusive use for RE business; simplified or actual method
deductions.passive_losses: Limited to $25k if AGI <$100k; phases out $100-150k AGI; suspended losses carry forward or released on sale
